docs.title: RF Engineer Notebook
docs.text:
Welcome! This is a living notebook for RF/MW design notes, simulations, and measurements.

Tip: Use the left navigation to browse topics. Use the top search bar to find anything quickly.

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Transmission Lines → Microstrip, CPW, GCPW
  • Simulation Techniques → ADS tips, CILD, EM setup checklists
  • Test & Measurement → VNA calibration, TDR notes

docs.title: Example Diagram (Mermaid)
docs.text:
flowchart LR
  A[Define Specs] --> B[Build Stackup]
  B --> C[EM Model]
  C --> D[Extract S-params]
  D --> E[Optimize/Match]
  E --> F[Validate on VNA]

docs.title: Example Math
docs.text: Sed ut perspiciatis $$ Z_0 = \sqrt{\dfrac{R + j\omega L}{G + j\omega C}} $$ with frequency-dependent elements for wideband work.

docs.title: Example Plot
docs.text: Made with MkDocs Material. Edit these pages in docs/ and push changes — the site updates automatically.

docs.title: LC Equivalents
docs.text:
Parallel/series equivalents used frequently in matching:

  • Parallel capacitors: \(C_{eq} = C_1 + C_2\)
  • Series capacitors: \(C_{eq} = \left( rac{1}{C_1} + rac{1}{C_2} ight)^{-1}\)
  • Parallel resistors: \(R_{eq} = \left( rac{1}{R_1} + rac{1}{R_2} ight)^{-1}\)
  • Series resistors: \(R_{eq} = R_1 + R_2\)
  • Parallel inductors: \(L_{eq} = \left( rac{1}{L_1} + rac{1}{L_2} ight)^{-1}\)
  • Series inductors: \(L_{eq} = L_1 + L_2\)

docs.title: ADS Workflow (Example)
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
  participant U as You
  participant A as ADS
  participant E as EM Solver
  U->>A: Define tech/stackup
  A->>E: Meshed geometry
  E-->>A: S-parameters, fields
  U->>A: Optimization goals
  A-->>U: Optimized layout

docs.title: Using CILD in ADS (Starter)
docs.text:
1. Define the stackup carefully (dielectric, metal, loss)
  2. Use CILD to sweep length/width/clearances
  3. Export S‑parameters and validate in circuit sims
Inputs: length (mm), width (µm), gap (µm)
Outputs: Z0, neff, IL (dB/mm)

docs.title: VNA Calibrations (Quick Notes)
docs.text:
• SOLT for coax; TRL for on‑wafer
  • Validate with line length delta at target bands (e.g., 80/140/220 GHz)

docs.title: DC Feed Designs (Thin‑Film to PCB)
docs.text:
• Use RC feed networks for bias stability
  • Keep return paths short; decouple at multiple decades
Example: 10 Ω // 100 pF at chip pad, 1 kΩ // 10 nF at TF edge, 10 µH + 100 nF on PCB

docs.title: Microstrip → CPW Transition
docs.text:
Goals: - Broadband 50 Ω from DC to 220 GHz - Minimal parasitics, controlled reference shift

Practical guidance

  • Taper widths gradually (<= 10° equivalent angle)
  • Keep reference plane transitions short and symmetric

docs.title: CPW Notes (No Backside Ground)
docs.text:
Key points: - Minimize slotline mode by symmetry and periodic bridges (if allowed) - Use gradual bends; avoid abrupt discontinuities to reduce mode conversion

Layout sketch:

docs.title: Microstrip Notes
docs.text:
• Substrate: define \(\epsilon_r\), thickness h, conductor thickness t
  • Target: 50 Ω wideband from DC → 220 GHz (manufacturing limits apply)

Design checklist

  • Confirm ground plane width and clearance
  • Model conductor surface roughness (if relevant)
  • Account for finish (Cu / ENIG / EPAG) in high‑fidelity sims
